Name: information_theoretic_compression
Title: Exploring the Information-Theoretic Limits of Neural Network Compression with Distortion Constraints
Short Hypothesis: We hypothesize that the fundamental limits of compressing neural networks can be characterized using rate-distortion theory, and that these limits can be achieved using a combination of quantization, pruning, and coding techniques.
Related Work: Recent works such as [1], [2], and [3] have explored the use of information-theoretic principles for compression and quantization. However, these works have focused on specific applications or techniques, rather than exploring the fundamental limits of compression. Our proposal builds on these works by establishing a general framework for characterizing the information-theoretic limits of neural network compression with distortion constraints.
Abstract: This proposal explores the information-theoretic limits of compressing neural networks with distortion constraints. We aim to establish a general framework for characterizing the fundamental limits of compression, and to develop techniques for achieving these limits using a combination of quantization, pruning, and coding techniques. Our approach is based on rate-distortion theory, which provides a powerful tool for analyzing the trade-off between compression rate and distortion. We will use this framework to derive bounds on the achievable rate-distortion performance of neural networks, and to develop practical coding schemes that approach these limits.
Experiments: Derive bounds on the achievable rate-distortion performance of neural networks using rate-distortion theory
Develop practical coding schemes for compressing neural networks, including quantization, pruning, and entropy coding techniques
Evaluate the performance of these coding schemes on a range of neural network architectures and tasks, including image classification, language modeling, and reinforcement learning
Risk Factors and Limitations: The complexity of the rate-distortion optimization problem may limit the scalability of our approach to large neural networks
The choice of distortion metric may significantly impact the results, and a poor choice may limit the effectiveness of our approach
The development of practical coding schemes that achieve the theoretical limits may require significant computational resources and expertise
